title: Warren v0.1.0: manual run path end-to-end
description:
Ship Warren v0.1.0 — the manual run path end-to-end. User can: connect canopy, add project, spawn run, watch live event tail, steer, cancel, see expertise capture round-trip. Single-user, single-host, docker compose deploy.

Out of scope (V2): scheduler, GitHub webhooks, library API exports.

Authoritative source: SPEC.md (V1 sections — see §3.1 V1 goals, §4.3 composition flow, §11.B resolved decisions).

Stack locked: Bun + TS strict, Bun.serve, bun:sqlite + Drizzle, React + Vite + shadcn/ui + Tailwind, supervisor as small Bun parent (§10.3), HttpClient from @os-eco/burrow.

Burrow dependency: shipped (commit 7926a0e on 2026-05-08); all 21 routes warren needs are live.

title: Phase 14 — End-to-end acceptance harness (boot real container, dispatch a real claude-code or sapling run against a sample project, verify §3.1 V1 goals + §11.A reap roundtrip + restart-recovery)
description: Originally framed as the release gate for v0.1.0 (§3.1 V1 goals + §11.A reap + restart-recovery). v0.1.0 was instead validated via SPEC §11.E first-run on 2026-05-09 — a manual real-claude-code run that completed end-to-end (state:succeeded, branchPushed:true) and surfaced three burrow-side gaps shipped in the same session: burrow-7b97 (dispatcher wiring), burrow-55e3 (toolchain forwarding via agents:[agent.name]), burrow-0329 (bwrap --uid/--gid). The harness's role is now regression-prevention for the 0.1.x line — automating the §11.E path so future patches don't silently regress it. Burrow-cli floor is now 0.2.3 (resolves the original Risk #1 in pl-9836). New V1 limitation seeds (warren-dcf3 / warren-93ee / warren-fab1 / warren-3c40 / warren-bd69) interact with specific scenarios — see child seeds warren-c37e and warren-392b for caveats. Stale risks in the original plan: #1 (burrow CLI version drift) is resolved by the 0.2.3 pin; #5 (quality gates exclude scripts/acceptance/) is stale per d7a788e — biome already enforces the tree.

title: Switch @os-eco/burrow-cli dep from file:../burrow to a published semver range once burrow is on npm
description: Phase 2 (warren-c0e6) wired @os-eco/burrow-cli via a file: reference because burrow isn't published yet. When it lands on npm, flip package.json to a semver range and re-run bun install. Touch package.json + bun.lock; verify HttpClient import + type still resolves; make sure CI publish workflows don't trip on the file: dep.

title: Scenarios 09+10 — reap roundtrip (per-run .mulch merges into project .mulch with LWW; seeds the agent closed land in project's .seeds; mulch.record.updated/skipped events visible)
description:
Post-0.1.0 caveats from SPEC §11.E first-run validation:

- warren-3c40: reap currently classifies 'queued, never started' as state:failed indistinguishably from a real agent crash (same observable shape: branchPushed:true, errors:[], state:failed). The stub agent must reach 'running' state before warren reaps it. Either set WARREN_STUB_SLEEP_MS generously enough that the dispatcher transitions queued→running before reap fires, or wait for warren-3c40's fix and assert against the post-fix shape (separate 'never_started' vs 'crashed' classification on the run record / reap event).

- warren-dcf3: the reap branch_push step requires git credential helper for github.com, which the container doesn't auto-wire from GITHUB_TOKEN. The harness should either (a) reuse the GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL insteadOf trick the projects scenario already uses, pointing pushes at a local bare repo fixture, or (b) accept a reap_failed event for branch_push and assert only the .mulch merge / .seeds close roundtrip.

title: Scenarios 11+12 — doctor exit codes + supervisor restart-budget (warren doctor exits 0 healthy / non-zero with hint when broken; supervisor restarts burrow within budget; warren tracked runs survive burrow restart)

title: Container mode + scenario 13 quality gates + ACCEPTANCE.md runbook (wire --container compose-based booting; bun test && lint && typecheck green; document --real, fly deploy, UI smoke manual gates; ml learn / sd sync / sd close warren-3ee3)
description:
Post-0.1.0 caveats from SPEC §11.E first-run validation:

- warren-fab1: warren CLI is not on container PATH. Container scenarios that shell 'warren <cmd>' must use 'bun /app/src/cli/main.ts <cmd>' as a workaround until the symlink (or 'bun link' in /app) lands. Document this in ACCEPTANCE.md alongside the workaround.

- warren-93ee: supervisor has no --no-auth knob for burrow. Container mode must set a real BURROW_API_TOKEN (and matching WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN) in the test compose env; loopback-only dev shortcuts won't work until WARREN_BURROW_NO_AUTH / WARREN_BURROW_ARGS lands.

- warren-bd69: runtime image lacks curl. Container-side HTTP probes need 'bun -e fetch(...)' instead of curl.

- Quality gates: biome already enforces scripts/acceptance/ as of d7a788e (the post-0.1.0 lint cleanup that unblocked CI). Harness code must pass 'bun run lint' from day one — the original plan Risk #5 ('quality gates exclude scripts/acceptance/') is stale.

- Release wiring: .github/workflows/release.yml (eb1a7ca) auto-tags and cuts a GitHub release on every VERSION bump. Add to ACCEPTANCE.md: the harness must be green before bumping VERSION, or a broken release ships unattended.

title: Supervisor can't pass --no-auth to burrow serve (no env knob)
description: src/supervisor/main.ts:364 hardcodes `burrow serve --socket <path>` with no way to inject --no-auth or extra args. Result: warren refuses to boot without BURROW_API_TOKEN even on a loopback-only dev box. Add WARREN_BURROW_NO_AUTH=1 (boolean) and/or WARREN_BURROW_ARGS (array) env knobs that resolveCommandFromEnv splices into burrowCmd. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09 — burrow crash-looped with 'BURROW_API_TOKEN is not set' until a token was set in .env.

title: AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS locks SPA out of its own Login.tsx (chicken-and-egg)
description: handlers.ts:506 sets AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS = new Set(['/healthz']). Hitting GET / with no Authorization header returns 401, but the SPA in src/ui/src/pages/Login.tsx is the *only* way for a user to enter the bearer token. Net effect: warren's UI is unreachable from a browser without the token already in scope. Fix: exempt /, /index.html, /assets/<hash>, and any SPA deep-link routes from the auth check (or move the auth gate to wrap only API routes, not the UI handler). Switch the data structure from Set<string> to a predicate so prefix matching is cheap. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: warren CLI not on PATH inside the container
description: Dockerfile installs four os-eco CLIs globally via 'bun install -g' but does not put warren itself on PATH. Result: 'docker compose exec warren warren register-agent ...' fails with 'executable file not found in PATH'. Workaround is 'docker compose exec warren bun /app/src/cli/main.ts <subcmd>'. Fix: either symlink ./src/cli/main.ts into /usr/local/bin/warren during the runtime stage, or run 'bun link' in /app so the package.json bin entries resolve. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: First-class local canopy support in docker-compose.yml
description: Smoke-testing warren without a real GitHub canopy repo requires manually adding a bind mount to docker-compose.yml so 'CANOPY_REPO_URL=file:///canopy-source' resolves. Lift that into the canonical compose file behind a CANOPY_SOURCE_DIR env var (already wired locally) and document it in README + .env.example. Lowers the bar for first-run testing and avoids requiring a public repo for a smoke test. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: Reap classifies 'queued, never started' as 'failed' indistinguishably from real crashes
description: Warren's spawn flow dispatches a run to burrow, then reaps it immediately. If the agent prompt is under-specified (no runtime/agent section in canopy frontmatter, empty burrow_config), burrow accepts the dispatch but never transitions the run from 'queued' to 'running'. Warren's reap then marks the warren-side run as state:failed even though branchPushed:true and errors:[] — the same observable shape as a real agent crash. Distinguish: (a) burrow run never started (config/runtime issue), (b) started and crashed (agent fault), (c) started and timed out. Surface this in the reap event and run record. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09 (run_ew47b7fzrj25).
closeReason: Added runs.failure_reason column (never_started|crashed|timed_out). Reap infers from warren state on entry: queued⇒never_started, running⇒crashed; overrideable for future deadline reapers. Surfaced on run row, reap.completed event, and RunDetail UI.

title: GITHUB_TOKEN not auto-wired into git credentials inside the container
description: The reap step runs 'git push' for the burrow branch but the container has no credential helper configured for github.com. GITHUB_TOKEN is in env_passthrough territory but never lands in git's credential store. Workaround: 'docker compose exec warren git config --global url.https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/.insteadOf https://github.com/' — non-durable across recreates. Fix: have the supervisor (or a small entrypoint shim) install the insteadOf rule from GITHUB_TOKEN at startup, before spawning burrow + warren. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09 — first run failed branch_push, second run with manual config succeeded (branchPushed:true).

title: Add curl (or wget) to the runtime image for diagnostics
description: Debugging the burrow socket required bun -e fetch() workarounds because the runtime image has no curl/wget. ~1MB cost, saves hours on first-run diagnostics. Add 'curl' to the apt-get install line in the runtime stage of Dockerfile. Found during first-run smoke test on 2026-05-09.

title: burrow serve has no run executor — warren runs queued forever
description: Warren's spawn flow dispatches a run via POST /burrows/:id/runs (burrow's HTTP API), which calls repos.runs.enqueue() — a DB insert with state=queued. NOTHING in burrow's startServer() instantiates a RunLoop or any executor. Runs sit in the DB indefinitely. Confirmed by reading: src/server/server.ts (no RunLoop instantiation), src/cli/commands/prompt.ts comment 'we deliberately bypass RunLoop — that machinery is for daemonized callers driving N burrows in parallel' (so prompt-the-CLI is the only inline driver), src/cli/main.ts comment 'V1 daemon-less'. Demonstrated empirically on 2026-05-09: /runs/run_myajgag76evw stayed at state:queued, startedAt:null after warren dispatch; running burrow prompt CLI directly with --agent claude-code created a fresh run that DID execute inline (started, failed at bwrap exec — separate toolchain issue). Fix: either (a) startServer instantiates a RunLoop and the warren container runs the executor in the same process, (b) warren spawns 'burrow prompt <id>' as a child process per dispatch, or (c) warren embeds RunLoop using burrow's exported class. (a) is the cleanest. This is a P0 blocker for warren V1 — without it warren cannot execute any agent run.
closeReason: Moved to burrow-7b97 — the fix lives in burrow's startServer (instantiate RunLoop) or its CLI surface, not in warren. Tracking continues there.

title: Toolchain auto-detection misses /usr/local/bin/claude in the runtime image
description: Burrow's collectToolchainPaths returns [] for a burrow whose canopy agent is named 'claude-code', even though /usr/local/bin/claude exists in the warren container. Result: bwrap sandbox spawns 'claude' and gets 'execvp claude: No such file or directory'. Demonstrated by burrow prompt bur_71v3sjxe31x3 --agent claude-code on 2026-05-09 — first event was stderr 'bwrap: execvp claude: No such file or directory', run failed with exitCode:1 in 486ms. Workarounds to investigate: (a) declare [[agents]] in burrow.toml so collectToolchainPaths walks the agent's expected bin paths, (b) extend symlink walker to inspect /usr/local/bin (bun install -g symlinks land there). Belongs in burrow but blocks warren's claude-code agent path.
closeReason: Moved to burrow-55e3. Body itself noted 'Belongs in burrow but blocks warren's claude-code agent path.' — collectToolchainPaths is burrow code; HttpBurrowUpInput change is burrow surface; warren-side wire-through is a thin follow-up. Tracking in burrow's seeds keeps ownership with the code.

title: Run status stuck on 'queued' in UI while agent is actively working
description:
While dogfooding warren on warren-9f65, the UI run view displayed status 'queued' even though the agent was actively running (events streaming, work happening).

Could be either:
- UI bug: misreads/maps the run status from /runs/:id (e.g., not advancing past 'queued' when burrow reports 'running')
- Backend bug: warren never transitions runs.status from 'queued' → 'running' when burrow's run-state reaches running, so the API returns stale state
- Burrow reporting bug: burrow doesn't emit a state-change event warren listens for

Repro:
1. docker compose up -d --build
2. Log in via UI (WARREN_AUTH_TOKEN)
3. Refresh agents, add warren as a project, create a run targeting warren-9f65
4. Observe: run streams events, but the status badge stays 'queued'

Investigation hints:
- Check src/api/runs.ts (status field source) vs src/db schema for runs.status transitions
- Check supervisor/burrow event handler for run.state.* events
- UI: src/ui/src/pages (run detail) — confirm it isn't pinned to initial status from list view

title: Run event-stream bridge dies on Bun.serve 10s idleTimeout, never reconnects → UI looks 'stuck'
description:
Warren's bridge that tails burrow's run events and forwards them to clients on /runs/:id/events drops the connection with ECONNRESET after ~10s of quiet, then ends and never recovers. Run continues in burrow but the UI sees no further events and no terminal status — appears stuck.

Logs (run bur_69zfr73rg82b, 2026-05-09):
  [Bun.serve]: request timed out after 10 seconds. Pass `idleTimeout` to configure.
  [Bun.serve]: request timed out after 10 seconds. Pass `idleTimeout` to configure.
  err='burrow unreachable at unix:/var/run/burrow.sock (ECONNRESET)' written=20 msg='run stream bridge errored'
  msg='run stream bridge ended'
  ... agent kept running in burrow, finalized 'succeeded' ~3min later. UI never knew.

Root causes (one or both):
1. warren's server (src/server/main.ts) calls Bun.serve without overriding the default 10s idleTimeout, so long-lived NDJSON SSE-style streams get killed
2. warren's burrow client uses HTTP-over-unix and the underlying Bun fetch hits the same idleTimeout while waiting for the next event chunk

Fixes:
- Set idleTimeout: 0 (or a large value) on warren's Bun.serve for the events route
- Same for the burrow client when consuming /runs/:id/events
- Add reconnect-on-error in the bridge with seq-based resume so a transient drop doesn't lose events

Related: warren-865e (UI shows 'queued' while agent works) — same root cause, since runs.status updates flow through the bridge.

title: UI overflows horizontally — page requires horizontal scrolling at common widths
description:
The warren UI doesn't fit horizontally on the screen. User reports needing to scroll horizontally to view everything.

Likely causes:
- A page or table without min-width: 0 / overflow-x: auto on the right scroll container
- Fixed-width columns that exceed viewport (runs list, projects list, events table)
- Sidebar + main pane summing to wider than viewport on smaller screens
- Missing max-w-* or w-full constraint on a page-level container

Repro:
1. docker compose up -d --build && open http://localhost:8080
2. Log in, navigate to (likely) Runs or Projects page
3. Observe horizontal scrollbar / clipped content

Investigation:
- src/ui/src/App.tsx layout shell
- src/ui/src/pages/* — check for w-screen / fixed widths / table overflow
- Browser devtools: find the element wider than 100vw

title: Run workspace is stale — agents work on outdated commit, missing recent changes from origin
description:
Warren creates a per-run workspace (e.g. /root/.local/share/burrow/projects/warren/workspaces/bur_<id>) but it does NOT reflect the current origin/<default-branch>. Discovered during dogfooding warren-9f65 on 2026-05-09 (run bur_69zfr73rg82b):

- Host warren repo HEAD: f515d54 (origin/main up-to-date)
- Run workspace HEAD: 2150f40 — eight commits behind, missing the entire scripts/acceptance/ harness
- Result: agent tasked with 'add scenario 04 alongside scenarios 01-03' had no scenarios/ directory to work in. Agent exited 0 having done nothing of substance.

Confirmed in code: src/projects/ has clone (on POST /projects) + rm-rf (on DELETE) only. NO fetch/pull/refresh path anywhere. /data/projects/<id>/ is frozen at registration time forever.

User feedback (must-fix framing):
  'I'm not going to create a new project for every PR/issue I work on,
   that defeats the whole purpose.'

Reference implementation: src/registry/clone.ts (canopy) already does this pattern correctly:
  // First refresh: git clone <repoUrl> <localDir>
  // Subsequent refreshes: git fetch && git reset --hard origin/<HEAD>

Same pattern needs to land for projects.

Design proposal:
1. Before each run's workspace build (POST /runs), fetch+reset the project clone to origin/<ref-or-default-branch>:
     git -C /data/projects/<id> fetch origin
     git -C /data/projects/<id> checkout <ref>
     git -C /data/projects/<id> reset --hard origin/<branch>
2. Accept optional 'ref' on POST /runs body so a run can target a specific commit/branch/tag
3. Add POST /projects/:id/refresh for explicit pull without spawning a run
4. UI: surface project's current HEAD sha + 'last fetched' timestamp on the projects page so users can sanity-check before kicking off a run
5. Persist last_fetched_at + last_head_sha on projects table

Repro:
1. Register a project via UI
2. Push new commits to the project's origin/main
3. Create a new run via UI — workspace HEAD will still be the registration-time commit
4. Agent silently does nothing (or worse, modifies stale code)

Acceptance:
- POST /runs against an existing project picks up new commits without DELETE+POST
- Existing acceptance scenario 03 (projects-management) extended to cover the refresh-on-run case
- Optional 'ref' param works with branch names and SHAs
- UI shows current project HEAD + age

title: DELETE /projects/:id fails with FK constraint when runs reference the project
description:
UI's delete-project confirm dialog promises 'Run history for this project is kept', but the schema declares runs.project_id as NOT NULL references(projects.id) with no onDelete clause (src/db/schema.ts:72-74). SQLite has FKs enabled, so deleting any project that has runs fails with 'FOREIGN KEY constraint failed'.

ESCALATION (2026-05-09 dogfood): the failure is not just 'can't delete' — it's a state-corrupting partial delete. deleteProject (src/projects/manage.ts:142-153) does rmrf(localPath) FIRST, then repo.delete(id). When the FK error throws on the second step, the disk is already gone but the projects row remains. Result: invariant from mx-be7a74 ('a projects row exists ⇒ its localPath exists on disk') is violated. Subsequent dispatches against that project fail with 'project clone missing on disk: /data/projects/...'. Two clicks (delete, then run) wedge the project; UI exposes no recovery path. Bumping to P1.

Recommended fix (option A, unchanged):
- runs.project_id nullable + onDelete: 'set null' on the FK; new migration rebuilds the runs table.
- UI renders orphaned runs (project_id null) on Runs list + RunDetail.
- Matches the dialog's existing promise.

Plus fix the operation ordering even after the FK is corrected:
- deleteProject should do repo.delete(id) FIRST inside a transaction, only then rmrf the disk. If the row delete fails (any reason), disk is preserved and the operator can retry. SPEC §11.E first-run notes (mx-be7a74) explicitly chose the current order to maintain a different invariant (no row without disk), but with onDelete: 'set null' that invariant becomes weaker — the row-first order is safer.
- Wrap the row delete + disk rmrf in something with rollback/recovery semantics (transaction over the row, best-effort rmrf with audit-log entry on failure). At minimum, never leave the system in 'row exists, disk gone'.

runs.agent_name → agents.name has the same shape — file a sibling if/when agent-delete is wired.

title: claude-code agent fails 'Not logged in' — no env-pass path for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in warren→burrow seam
description:
Second dogfood run (run_hkkm35bcckc4, 2026-05-09) failed immediately: claude-code launched in the sandbox, init event fired, then 'Not logged in · Please run /login' and exit. The auth path that's supposed to make this work is broken end-to-end:

1. burrow's claude-code runtime forwards host credentials via forwardClaudeHostCredentials (node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/runtime/claude-code.ts:119): macOS Keychain on darwin, ~/.claude/.credentials.json on linux. Inside the warren container the runtime user is the bun process — its ~/.claude is empty, no Keychain access. OAuth path is dead by construction.
2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY fallback would work IF burrow's sandbox forwarded it, but burrow gates env-forwarding on the project's burrow.toml [env] section. Warren has no burrow.toml.
3. The .env.example (lines 30-32) and SPEC §11.D claim 'mount these into the agent runtime via canopy env_passthrough in the agent's burrow.toml', but parseBurrowConfig (src/runs/burrow_config.ts:32) only reads [sandbox].network — env_passthrough is silently ignored. Docs claim a feature that doesn't exist.

Primary fix is burrow-side (filed as burrow-e9e7): default-allow ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (+ OAuth env names) in the built-in claude-code runtime without requiring project burrow.toml [env]. claude-code is a built-in runtime — its env contract should be built-in too.

Warren-side TODO once burrow lands the fix:
- Decide whether to also wire canopy-level env_passthrough through burrows.up (extend HttpBurrowUpInput, parseBurrowConfig). If we don't, delete the .env.example claim + SPEC line so docs match reality.
- Reap should classify 'agent exited 1 with no model response' as a credential/runtime failure_reason, not a generic 'crashed' (related to warren-3c40 — the discriminator added there only distinguishes never_started/crashed/timed_out today).

Why the first dogfood (warren-5h ago) appeared to succeed is unclear — possibly a manually mounted .claude/ or different canopy frontmatter. The clean dogfood path hits this hole.

title: Run stays in 'running' state after agent emits terminal result event
description:
Second dogfood run (run_hkkm35bcckc4, 2026-05-09) is permanently stuck in state:running even though claude-code exited 14ms after launch. The bridge captured the agent's terminal result event correctly:

- seq:1 system/init
- seq:2 stdout 'Not logged in · Please run /login'
- seq:3 system result {subtype:'result', is_error:true, terminal_reason:'completed', num_turns:1, duration_ms:14}
- seq:4 system cancel.requested {mode:'forwarded', burrowRunId:'run_2qsnyn57kjzz', burrowRunState:'failed'}  ← added after a UI cancel attempt

GET /runs/run_hkkm35bcckc4 still returns state:'running', endedAt:null, failureReason:null. The seq:4 event is the smoking gun: warren's cancel handler synchronously observed burrowRunState:'failed' (i.e., burrow KNOWS the run is terminal) and still didn't transition the warren row. UI's runs list and RunDetail show 'running' indefinitely.

Per mx-fadaa2 ('cancelRun forwards to burrow without transitioning warren state: Reap is the only path that finalizes a non-terminal warren run'), this is by design — but reap clearly isn't running. Either:
1. Reap is not wired to fire on bridge-stream-close OR on a cancel that observed terminal burrow state.
2. Reap is polling but not finding/processing this run.
3. Reap exists only as code, not actually scheduled in this server build.

Recommended fix path (now with stronger signal):
- In cancelRun: when the burrow response carries burrowRunState in {succeeded,failed,cancelled}, trigger reap for that warren run synchronously (or finalize inline using the burrow state). The handler already has the answer — no reason to bounce through a poll loop.
- In bridgeRunStream: when an event with the runtime's terminal shape arrives (claude-code: kind=state_change, payload.type=result, payload.terminal_reason set), trigger reap for that run synchronously.
- These two short-circuits eliminate the dependency on a separate reap scheduler for the common cases (agent exits cleanly OR operator cancels a stuck run).

UX impact: dogfooding looks broken — a failed run looks like a hung run AND clicking cancel looks like nothing happened. Compounds the warren-5165 / burrow-e9e7 issue: even after burrow lands the credential fix, any future agent crash will look 'running' forever and cancel won't recover it.

Diagnostic IDs: bur_b2h3915n17mk, burrow run run_2qsnyn57kjzz (burrow already has it as 'failed'). Sibling UI bug filed separately for the badge not reacting to incoming events.

title: RunDetail UI doesn't react to incoming events — state badge stale, cancel feels like a no-op
description:
After clicking 'cancel' on a stuck run in the UI (run_hkkm35bcckc4, 2026-05-09), nothing visibly changed: no badge update, no toast, no spinner, no transition. The only feedback was a new event landing in the run's terminal output: seq:4 cancel.requested system {mode:'forwarded', burrowRunId:'run_2qsnyn57kjzz', burrowRunState:'failed'}. The event stream was working — the UI just didn't reflect it.

Two layers of the issue:

1. The state badge on RunDetail (and the row on /runs) reads from a one-shot GET /runs/:id, not from useEventStream. When a terminal/cancel/state-change event arrives, the page doesn't re-fetch. Even after warren-a69a (reap finalization) is fixed, the UI will still show stale state until the user refreshes.

2. POST /runs/:id/cancel returns and the click handler... does what? No optimistic UI, no toast on response, no error if already-terminal. Operator can't tell whether the click registered, whether burrow accepted it, whether warren acknowledged anything. They only see the cancel event in the stream because they happen to be watching it.

Recommended scope:
- useEventStream observers re-trigger a GET /runs/:id when an event with kind in {state_change, cancel.requested, reap.completed} (or any system terminal-shape) arrives. Cheap, simple, covers all the run-finalization paths. Alternative: derive run state directly from the event log, but that's a bigger refactor.
- Cancel button: optimistic disabled state on click, success/error toast on response, surface burrowRunState if returned. If the response indicates burrow is already terminal, immediately re-fetch the run (or, better, the cancel handler finalizes inline per warren-a69a and the re-fetch shows the new state).

Companion to warren-a69a (server-side reap gap). This sd is purely the UI's reactivity to events that DO arrive correctly.

title: os-eco CLIs (sd/ml/cn/sapling/overstory) are dangling symlinks at runtime — installed to /root by build, executed as user 'bun'
description:
Second dogfood retry (run_rpe5tvs06bpg, 2026-05-09) auth path now works (apiKeySource: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in init event — burrow 0.2.6 fix landed cleanly), but the agent immediately tripped on a different DX hole: the os-eco CLIs are dangling symlinks at runtime.

The symlinks point at /root's bun store:
  /usr/local/bin/sd -> ../../../root/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@os-eco/seeds-cli/src/index.ts
  /usr/local/bin/ml -> ../../../root/.bun/install/global/node_modules/@os-eco/mulch-cli/src/cli.ts

But the runtime container drops to user 'bun', and /root is mode 0700 (root-only). So 'which sd' resolves, exec fails ('No such file or directory' even though the symlink itself exists). Confirmed during the same run that 'cn' (canopy-cli) is also broken: the agent's own self-report (run summary, seq:211) says 'Couldn't run the harness end-to-end here — cn (canopy CLI) isn't on PATH in this sandbox, so the fixture builder fails before any scenario runs.' Almost certainly affects sapling and overstory too — same install path.

Why this is happening: Dockerfile's 'bun install -g <pkgs>' (around line 48) runs as root (the default build user). bun installs binaries into root's home (/root/.bun/install/global/) and creates global symlinks under /usr/local/bin pointing at root's home. The runtime stage then USERs to 'bun' (or the supervisor drops privileges) — bun can't traverse /root, the symlinks dangle.

Repro inside the running container:
  docker compose exec warren whoami        # bun
  docker compose exec warren which sd      # /usr/local/bin/sd
  docker compose exec warren sd --version  # bash: ...: No such file or directory
  docker compose exec warren ls -la /root  # drwx------ ... (denied)

Recommended fix:
- Run 'bun install -g' as the runtime user (USER bun) during the build, so the bun store lives in /home/bun/.bun and is world-traversable. Or set BUN_INSTALL=/usr/local before the install so the store lives in /usr/local/lib/bun and the symlinks land relative to /usr/local/bin (no cross-user traversal).
- Either way: 'docker compose exec warren sd --help', 'ml --help', 'cn --help', 'sapling --help' should all succeed for the runtime user, and inside the sandbox after a fresh burrow up.

Acceptance: a fresh 'warren run claude-code <project> -p "sd ready"' shows real sd output in the run events; same for ml prime, cn list.

Impact during this dogfood: the agent's prompt explicitly said 'use ml', the agent tried 'ml prime', got 'command not found', and gave up on the mulch+seeds workflow entirely. It produced 102 turns of work without ever priming or recording. Worse: 'cn' being broken means the acceptance harness can't run end-to-end inside the sandbox, so an agent working on warren's own acceptance scenarios can't validate them. Affects every os-eco-CLI-dependent workflow.

Found: 2026-05-09 dogfood retry, run_rpe5tvs06bpg, seq:2-21 (sd/ml symptoms), seq:211 (cn confirmation in the agent's own summary).

title: Burrow worktree gitdir is unreachable from inside the sandbox — agent can't run git inside its own working tree
description:
Second dogfood retry (run_rpe5tvs06bpg, 2026-05-09): the agent did 12m48s of real work and emitted a clean terminal event — but the warren burrow worktree's .git file points to '/data/projects/jayminwest/warren/.git/worktrees/bur_b9r7j5evbcg0', and that path is OUTSIDE the bwrap bind mount. From inside the sandbox at /workspace, the gitdir reference dangles. Agent's own note (seq:211): 'sd sync / ml sync / git push deferred — .git in this worktree points to a missing gitdir, so git commands fail.'

Consequences:
1. Agent can't 'git status' / 'git add' / 'git commit' inside the sandbox. So even when the agent finishes real work, there's no commit in the burrow worktree for warren's reap step to push.
2. Combined with warren-a69a (reap doesn't transition), this means: even if reap were fixed today, branchPushed would still be false because the worktree has nothing to push. Today's run produced zero remote branches despite a successful agent run.
3. Expected by SPEC §11.E ('branchPushed: true' was the first-run success criterion), so this is a regression from the first dogfood — either burrow's worktree layout changed or the bind mount changed between burrow 0.2.3 and 0.2.6. Worth bisecting against the working first run if reproducing matters.

Architecturally this is a burrow issue (burrow owns sandboxing + worktree creation), but it surfaces as a warren-side workflow gap — agents running in warren can't commit, can't push, can't 'sd sync', can't 'ml sync'. The §4.3 composition flow assumes a working git inside the sandbox.

Three plausible fixes (in burrow, or warren↔burrow seam):
- Bind-mount '<project>/.git/worktrees/<burrowId>' into the sandbox alongside '/workspace' so the gitdir dereferences.
- Materialize a real '.git' directory inside the worktree instead of using the .git-file-pointing-at-parent layout. Costs disk on every burrow up but eliminates the cross-mount reference.
- Bind-mount the entire project's '/data/projects/<owner>/<name>/.git' into the sandbox at the same relative location. Simplest, also exposes the parent worktree refs to the agent (might be desirable: 'git log origin/main' should work).

Cross-cuts with warren-a69a (reap not transitioning) and warren-1eaa (sd/ml/cn dangling). Today's open dogfood blockers, in order of priority:
- burrow-e9e7 (FIXED in 0.2.6 — credential plumbing)
- THIS ONE (P1 — agent literally cannot commit)
- warren-a69a (P2 — UI stuck running, reap missing)
- warren-1eaa (P2 — CLIs dangling)
- warren-d9ad (P3 — UI doesn't react to events)

Found: 2026-05-09, run_rpe5tvs06bpg, agent's own summary at seq:211. Burrow id bur_b9r7j5evbcg0 (the one with the broken .git pointer).
closeReason: Architecturally a burrow issue; tracked there as burrow-7a80. Fix lands in burrow's worktree+bwrap composition (bind-mount the host worktree gitdir into the sandbox, or materialize a real .git inside the worktree). Reopen on warren side only if a deps bump or warren-side coordination is needed once burrow ships the fix.
